---
title: "R Markdown for Reproducible Science"
subtitle: "Morning block of the NIEHS one-day workshop"
author: "R. Burke Squires"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output:
  ioslides_presentation:
    widescreen: true
    css: rmd_slides_style.css
    self_contained: true
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  echo = TRUE,
  message = FALSE,
  warning = FALSE,
  comment = "",
  fig.height = 2.8,
  fig.width = 6
)
```

## Today's durable outcome

By 5 PM, participants will render a Quarto HTML report from a clean session with narrative, an inline result, a table, a figure, a caption, a cross-reference, and a citation.

::: incremental
- R Markdown first: understand the model.
- Bridge: translate a safe copy.
- Quarto: continue one cumulative learner report.
:::

## Morning schedule

| Time | Focus |
|---|---|
| 09:00–09:15 | Preflight and learner workspace |
| 09:15–09:35 | YAML, Markdown, and knitting |
| 09:35–10:00 | Chunks and inline results |
| 10:00–10:15 | Break |
| 10:15–10:35 | R Markdown → Quarto bridge |

## Work in the learner directory

> Edit `student_work/morning_report.Rmd`, not the lesson or slide source.

The course website is read-only. Checkpoints in `student_work/checkpoints/` provide recovery without changing the instructor materials.

# R Markdown anatomy {.segue}

## Three parts

1. **YAML** — metadata and output settings.
2. **Markdown prose** — the scientific narrative.
3. **Executable code** — chunks and inline expressions.

> The source document records how the output was made.

## YAML

```yaml
---
title: "My Morning R Markdown Report"
author: "Your name"
date: "`r knitr::inline_expr('Sys.Date()')`"
output:
  html_document:
    toc: true
    code_folding: show
---
```

YAML is indentation-sensitive. Use spaces, not tabs.

## Markdown prose

| Source | Meaning |
|---|---|
| `**important**` | **important** |
| `*emphasis*` | *emphasis* |
| `## Methods` | A section heading |
| `- item` | A bullet |
| `[NIEHS](https://www.niehs.nih.gov)` | A link |

Use headings to represent structure, not merely visual size.

# Knitting {.segue}

## What happens

```text
.Rmd source
   ↓
knitr executes R and inserts results
   ↓
Pandoc creates HTML, DOCX, or PDF
```

## Clean-session principle

When the complete document knits:

- Required packages were loaded by the source.
- Required data were read by the source.
- Required objects were created in document order.

> A successful knit supports computational reproducibility; scientific validity still requires review.

# Hands-on {.segue}

## Edit the safe learner file

In `student_work/morning_report.Rmd`:

1. Change the title and author.
2. Add bold and italic text.
3. Replace the placeholder learning goals.
4. Knit.
5. Restart R and knit again.

Success: the file knits after restart and shows your edits.
